title: 10 Herbal Teas That Can Help Relieve an Itchy Scalp
intro_desc: An itchy scalp can be a real nuisance, causing discomfort and distraction throughout the day. If left untreated, it can lead to hair loss, skin irritation, and even anxiety. Fixing the issue is essential to regain confidence and feel more comfortable in your own skin. One effective way to alleviate an itchy scalp is by using herbal teas.  Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent that can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp. Its active ingredient, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp environment. By using a tea tree oil-based herbal tea, you can help control fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness.  Another herb that can help alleviate an itchy scalp is Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary. Rosemary has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and itching on the scalp. Its antioxidant properties can also help protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy hair growth.  To use herbal teas for an itchy scalp, you can steep a few sprigs of fresh rosemary or a few drops of tea tree oil in hot water, then let it cool before applying it to your scalp. Leave it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing it off with a gentle shampoo. You can also add a few drops of tea tree oil to your shampoo or conditioner for an added boost. By incorporating herbal teas into your scalp care routine, you can say goodbye to itchy scalp and hello to a healthier, happier you.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tea, also known as tea tree oil, is made from the leaves of a plant that grows in Australia. It has been used for many years to help with skin problems, including an itchy scalp. The tea tree oil in the tea has special powers that can help to calm down the skin on your scalp and stop the itchiness. It has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which means it can kill the tiny bugs and germs that can cause irritation and itchiness on your scalp. When you drink the tea or apply it to your scalp, it can help to reduce the inflammation and redness that often come with an itchy scalp. It can also help to soothe and calm the skin, making it less itchy and more comfortable. Some people find that using Melaleuca alternifolia tea helps to reduce dandruff and other scalp conditions that can cause itchiness.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins help to soothe itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and fighting off fungal infections that can cause irritation.
Quercetin in Aloe Vera is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and itching caused by irritation or infections.
Tannins are good for an itchy scalp because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin and reduce redness, which in turn provides relief from itchiness.
Kaempferol is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and soothe irritation, thereby providing relief from itchy and flaky scalp.
Polyphenols help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin, which can provide relief from irritation and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal teas for itchy scalp
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea for an itchy scalp, start by gathering a few ingredients: fresh Aloe vera leaves, dried peppermint leaves, and dried chamomile flowers. Choose a few large Aloe vera leaves, remove the outer skin, and chop them into small pieces. Combine these pieces with 1/4 cup of dried peppermint leaves and 1/4 cup of dried chamomile flowers in a pot. Pour in 2 cups of boiling water, cover the pot, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse. Strain the tea into a bowl to remove the solids, let it cool, and then use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. For a stronger tea, you can repeat the steeping process one or two more times. Once cooled, apply the tea to your scalp with a cotton ball, massage it gently, and leave it on for a few minutes before rinsing with warm water. Regular use of this Aloe vera herbal tea may help soothe an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a natural remedy for an itchy scalp. Rosemary has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness on the scalp, causing itchiness. It contains antioxidants that can soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. The tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties can also help control fungal and bacterial infections that may be causing the itchiness. By reducing inflammation and infections, rosemary tea can help clear up dandruff and other scalp conditions that lead to itchiness. Additionally, rosemary tea can help improve blood circulation to the scalp, which can promote healthy hair growth and reduce itchiness. Drinking rosemary tea may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to scalp issues.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for itchy scalp
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal tea that helps with an itchy scalp, start by cutting a piece of Aloe vera leaf from a mature plant. Remove the outer green layer, and scoop out the thick, gel-like substance inside. You will need about two to three tablespoons of Aloe vera gel. Add this gel to a pot of boiling water, about a liter of water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for about 10 to 15 minutes, allowing the Aloe vera to release its healing properties into the water. Once the tea has cooled down, you can strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. You can also add other herbs like tea tree oil or chamomile to enhance the tea's benefits. Finally, pour the Aloe vera tea over your scalp while it's still warm, and let it sit for about 15 to 20 minutes before rinsing it off with cold water. This soothing tea can help to calm an itchy scalp and promote a healthy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a natural remedy for an itchy scalp. The tea is made from the leaves of the nettle plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, making it less itchy. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. The antioxidants also help to soothe the skin and calm irritation. Additionally, nettle tea has antihistamine properties, which can help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by an allergic reaction. When applied topically or drunk as a tea, the compounds in nettle tea can help to relieve itchy scalp symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for people with scalp issues. Nettle tea can be used as a shampoo or applied directly to the scalp before shampooing, allowing its beneficial compounds to penetrate the skin.

remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for itchy scalp
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea for an itchy scalp, start by gathering a few ingredients. You will need aloe vera gel, dried peppermint leaves, dried chamomile flowers, and boiling water. Begin by washing your hands and aloe vera gel container with soap to prevent any bacteria from entering the gel. Next, scoop out about 2-3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel from the container and put it in a small saucepan. Add 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers to the saucepan. Pour in 2 cups of boiling water over the herbs and aloe vera gel, then reduce the heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes. Let the tea steep, allowing the flavors and benefits of the herbs and aloe vera to infuse into the water. Once the tea has steeped, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the solids. Finally, let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before applying it to your scalp using a cotton ball or a gentle massaging motion with your fingertips. The Aloe vera herbal tea should help soothe and calm an itchy scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce redness and swelling on the scalp. The tea also contains antioxidants, which help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. Aloe vera has a soothing effect on the skin, which can calm irritation and itching on the scalp. It also moisturizes the scalp, which can help to reduce dryness and flakiness. When applied to the scalp, aloe vera tea may help to balance the pH level, making it more comfortable for people with itchy scalp. The tea is also easy to make and can be applied directly to the scalp, making it a convenient treatment option.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones in Aloe Vera are good for itchy scalp because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm and soothe the irritated skin and scalp, reducing itching and promoting healthy hair growth.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for itchy scalp
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea for an itchy scalp, start by harvesting a few leaves of Aloe vera from your plant or buying them from a store. Wash the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Then, dry the leaves thoroughly with a clean towel to prevent any moisture from affecting the tea. Next, peel the outer green skin of the Aloe vera leaves to reveal the gel-filled inside. Scoop out the gel and blend it with 1 cup of boiling water in a blender or food processor until it's well mixed. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove any remaining gel or pulp. You can add a few drops of tea tree oil or a slice of lemon to the tea for extra benefits. Let the tea cool down before applying it to your scalp by massaging it gently with your fingertips. Leave the tea on for 15-20 minutes before washing it off with a mild shampoo. Repeat this process 1-2 times a week to help soothe an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis tea has been used for centuries to soothe an itchy scalp. The tea is made from the roots of the soapwort plant, which is rich in saponins - natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These saponins help to calm down irritated skin and reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can cause itching and discomfort. The tea also contains antioxidants that help to protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy hair growth. When applied topically, the saponins in Saponaria officinalis tea can help to remove dirt and oil from the scalp, reducing the risk of irritation and itchiness. Additionally, the tea's anti-fungal properties can help to combat scalp infections, such as dandruff and ringworm, which can cause itchy scalps. Regular use of Saponaria officinalis tea may help to reduce the frequency and severity of itchy scalp episodes, promoting a healthy and comfortable scalp.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea for an itchy scalp, start by taking 2-3 inner gel leaves of an Aloe vera plant and cut them into small pieces. Add 1 teaspoon of the Aloe gel to a cup of boiling water and reduce the heat to a low simmer for 5-7 minutes. Next, add a few leaves of fresh peppermint, 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, and a pinch of baking soda to the cup. Allow the mixture to steep for another 5 minutes, then strain it into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve. Let the tea cool down before using it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. This Aloe vera herbal tea can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy hair growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, is a natural remedy for an itchy scalp. The tea contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp. The anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from discomfort. Additionally, the tea has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help control the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause irritation and itchiness on the scalp. The tea also promotes healthy hair growth by improving blood circulation to the scalp, which can help reduce itchiness and promote a healthy scalp environment. Drinking Cymbopogon citratus tea regularly may help to alleviate itchy scalp issues and promote overall scalp health.

Use a spoon to scoop out the gel and place it in a small saucepan. Add one cup of water to the saucepan, making sure the water is not too hot. Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, allowing the water to absorb the healing properties of the Aloe vera gel. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool down. Once it has cooled, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the Aloe vera gel. You can now drink the tea or use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. To use it as a final rinse, pour the tea over your hair and scalp, massaging it gently into your scalp before rinsing with cool water. This tea is known to soothe and calm an itchy scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing irritation.

Remove the thick outer skin and take out the clear gel inside. Chop the gel into small pieces and add 2-3 tablespoons of it to a cup of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup. You can add a sweetener like honey if you like it sweet. Next, mix 1 teaspoon of dried tea leaves like peppermint, chamomile, or licorice root with the Aloe vera liquid. These herbs help soothe and calm the scalp. Let the tea cool down, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Apply it to your scalp, massage it in, and leave it on for a few minutes before rinsing it off with water. You can either buy an Aloe vera plant or get some gel from a store. Next, peel the outer skin of the Aloe vera gel, then chop it into small pieces. Repeat this process a few times a week for the best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album tea, also known as sandalwood tea, has been used for centuries to soothe an itchy scalp. The tea is made from the bark of the sandalwood tree, which contains a compound called santalol. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling on the scalp. When applied topically, the tea can help calm irritation and itchiness, promoting a healthy scalp environment. The antifungal properties of the tea can also help combat fungal infections that can cause itchiness, such as ringworm and dandruff. By reducing inflammation and fighting fungal infections, Santalum album tea can help provide relief from an itchy scalp and promote a healthy, balanced scalp ecosystem. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy hair growth.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea that helps with an itchy scalp, start by washing and drying a few leaves of Aloe vera. Then, chop the leaves into small pieces and put them in a pot with 2 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Next, strain the liquid into a cup, and let it cool down. Once it's cool, you can use it as a tea by drinking it or applying it to your scalp by dipping a cotton ball into the liquid and gently rubbing it on your scalp. When using the tea, massage it gently into your scalp for a few minutes before washing your hair with a mild shampoo, and you may start to feel relief from itchy scalp within a few days.

The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera also help to ease irritation and discomfort, making it easier to sleep and perform daily activities. Additionally, aloe vera tea has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to eliminate the root cause of the itchiness, such as fungal or bacterial infections. By drinking aloe vera tea or applying it to the scalp, you can help to calm the itchiness and promote a healthy scalp.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal teas for itchy scalp
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make aloe vera herbal tea that helps with an itchy scalp, you'll need to start by harvesting fresh aloe vera gel from the aloe plant. Cut off the thick green leaves from the plant and carefully remove the outer skin to reveal the clear, jelly-like gel inside. Scoop out about 2-3 tablespoons of the gel and place it in a saucepan with 2 cups of water. Heat the mixture over low heat, allowing the aloe gel to infuse into the water for about 10-15 minutes. Next, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool down. Once cooled, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and transfer the aloe vera tea to a bottle or jar. To use the tea, simply pour 1 cup of the liquid over your hair after shampooing and let it sit for 5-10 minutes before rinsing it out with warm water. Repeat this process once or twice a week to help soothe and calm your itchy scalp.

supplementary_best: To remedy an itchy scalp, a combination of Aloe vera, Urtica dioica, and Cymbopogon citratus teas is the most effective. Aloe vera is known for its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and redness. Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help eliminate scalp infections and balance the scalp's pH. Cymbopogon citratus, or lemongrass, contains antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce itchiness and prevent further irritation. By combining these three teas, you can create a potent remedy that addresses multiple aspects of an itchy scalp, from soothing inflammation to eliminating infections and preventing further irritation. To brew the tea, combine equal parts of each herb in boiling water, let it steep for 5-7 minutes, and strain before use. Apply the tea as a scalp treatment by massaging it into the scalp and leaving it on for 30 minutes to an hour before shampooing.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an itchy scalp, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. Chamomile tea is a good example. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties, but it can also cause skin irritation in some people. If you're sensitive to chamomile, drinking its tea may make your itchy scalp worse. Another tea to avoid is ginger tea. While ginger has many health benefits, it can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals, which may worsen an itchy scalp. Also, avoid drinking tea made from calendula, which is also known as marigold. Calendula can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some people, including those with an itchy scalp. You should also be cautious with tea made from Echinacea, which is often used to boost the immune system. However, Echinacea can cause skin reactions in some individuals, including those with an itchy scalp.
supplementary_other_remedies: For an itchy scalp, some people try using coconut oil. This oil has natural properties that help soothe and calm the scalp. When applied to the affected area, coconut oil can moisturize and reduce inflammation, making it an effective remedy for itchy scalps caused by dryness or irritation. Another natural remedy is apple cider vinegar. It has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help balance the scalp's pH and fight off infections that can cause itchiness. Some people also try using baking soda as a scalp mask. Baking soda helps remove dead skin cells and neutralize the scalp's pH, reducing itchiness and flakiness. Additionally, some people use aloe vera gel to calm and soothe an itchy scalp. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, making it a popular remedy for scalp issues. These natural remedies can be used in place of or in addition to shampoos and conditioners to help alleviate itchy scalp problems.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with an itchy scalp often experience other skin issues. Dandruff, a condition where dead skin cells flake off, is common. This is because the same fungus that causes dandruff, Malassezia, can also irritate the scalp and cause itchiness. Medicinal herbs like tea tree oil can help by killing the fungus.  Eczema, a condition where skin becomes inflamed and itchy, can also be triggered by an itchy scalp. This is because the itchiness can lead to scratching, which further irritates the skin and causes inflammation. Herbs like aloe vera can help soothe the skin and reduce inflammation.  Psoriasis, a condition where skin cells grow too quickly, can also cause an itchy scalp. This is because the rapid growth of skin cells can lead to itchiness and irritation. Herbs like turmeric contain curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce skin cell growth and soothe the scalp.  Folliculitis, an infection of the hair follicles, can also cause an itchy scalp. This is because the infection can lead to inflammation and irritation of the scalp. Herbs like neem can help by killing the bacteria that cause the infection.  Ringworm, a fungal infection that can cause a circular patch of itchy skin, can also cause an itchy scalp. Herbs like lavender can help by killing the fungus that causes the infection.
